touringPackageDescription: Peruvian electronic DJ/producer who performs energetic live sets blending club-ready house music with live instrumental elements. Her shows range from DJ sets to live performances with a bassist/percussionist, featuring joyful, emotionally resonant dance music drawn from her albums and EPs. She tours internationally at major festivals and club venues worldwide.

evidence.sourceName: Resident Advisor - Sofia Kourtesis Tour Dates
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Sofia Kourtesis has multiple 2026 tour dates including Bergtattfestivalen (Norway), PhillGood Festival, and UK dates at Finsbury Park and Collins Barracks Dublin.

evidence.sourceName: Ninja Tune - Sofia Kourtesis Events
evidence.evidenceSnippet: 2026 dates include Manchester (May 22), Brighton (June 6), Italy (July 31/Aug 1), London Finsbury Park (Aug 8), and Dublin (Aug 28).

evidence.sourceName: NME - Glastonbury 2022 Live Review
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Her 'live' set sees Kourtesis share the stage with a bassist and percussionist while she flits between kit and microphone with big rockstar energy. Infectiously joyful hour-long set of chilled-out house.

touringPackageDescription: Classic rock institution performing hit-filled 90-minute to 2-hour concerts at arenas, amphitheaters, and state fairs across North America. The band delivers a wall-to-wall hits show featuring classics like The Joker, Fly Like an Eagle, and Jet Airliner with Miller's signature storytelling and blues guitar work. Typically performs with a 6-piece band including keyboardists and vocalists.

evidence.sourceName: Pollstar - Steve Miller Band 2025 Tour Announcement
evidence.evidenceSnippet: 31-date tour Aug-Nov 2025 across US, playing theaters, amphitheaters, arenas, and state fairs. Band has 442 boxoffice reports dating back to 1999 with $121.8M gross and 2.5M tickets sold.

evidence.sourceName: Steve Miller Band Official - Tour Dates
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Active touring schedule with 2025 dates at Rose Theater NYC, and 2026 dates at Ironstone Amphitheatre. All-star band features top jazz and blues musicians.

evidence.sourceName: TickPick - Steve Miller Band 2025 Tour
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Setlist includes Fly Like an Eagle, The Joker, Rock n Me, Abracadabra, Take the Money and Run, Jet Airliner. Concerts last 90 minutes to 2 hours.

touringPackageDescription: The 89-year-old minimalist composer no longer performs personally, but his works are regularly performed by the Steve Reich Ensemble and other classical/new music groups worldwide. Performances feature large-scale minimalist compositions like Music for 18 Musicians and Drumming performed by chamber ensembles with percussion, strings, keyboards, and voices in concert hall settings. A major 90th birthday festival is scheduled for October 2026.

evidence.sourceName: The Glasshouse - Steve Reich at 90 Festival
evidence.evidenceSnippet: October 2026 festival in honor of Reich's 90th birthday features Colin Currie Group, Royal Northern Sinfonia, BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra performing works including Music for 18 Musicians, Drumming, The Desert Music.

evidence.sourceName: Steve Reich Official - Concerts
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Upcoming performances of Reich's works by Alarm Will Sound/Steve Reich Ensemble at Park Avenue Armory (Oct 2026).

evidence.sourceName: Wikipedia - Steve Reich and Musicians
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Founded in 1966, the Steve Reich Ensemble has premiered and performed many of Reich's works nationally and internationally. Group has performed at Carnegie Hall, Lincoln Center, and BAM.

touringPackageDescription: Progressive rock artist's live shows are immersive audio-visual experiences featuring two-set performances with his full band. The 2025 Overview Tour featured complete performances of his space-themed album with high-definition video projections and surround audio. Set one typically covers the new album in full; set two draws from his solo catalog and Porcupine Tree classics. Only two 2026 shows scheduled (Royal Albert Hall with orchestra).

evidence.sourceName: XS Noize - Steven Wilson 2026 Shows
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Only 2026 live shows: two nights at Royal Albert Hall (Oct 28-29) with Parallax Orchestra and choir, performing arrangements of The Overview tracks.

evidence.sourceName: Steven Wilson HQ - The Overview Tour 2025
evidence.evidenceSnippet: First tour in 7 years. Audio-visual experience based around space-themed album The Overview. Solo band includes Randy McStine (guitar), Adam Holzman (keyboards), Nick Beggs (bass), Craig Blundell (drums).

evidence.sourceName: Louder Sound - Live Review Stockholm 2025
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Two distinct halves separated by intermission. First set: nine tracks from solo catalog. Second set: complete The Overview album with vivid animated film projections on massive hi-def screen. Described as interstellar sound voyage with Floydian grandeur.

touringPackageDescription: Rock icon performs arena and theater shows blending Fleetwood Mac classics (Dreams, Rhiannon, Landslide) with solo hits (Edge of Seventeen, Stand Back). Her concerts feature elaborate stage production with video screens displaying tributes to musical legends, storytelling between songs, and her signature mystical stage presence. Performs with a full backing band, typically 13 songs plus encores in a nearly two-hour show.

evidence.sourceName: Ticketmaster - Stevie Nicks 2026 Tour Dates
evidence.evidenceSnippet: 2026 dates include Moody Center Austin (April 22) and New Orleans Jazz & Heritage Festival (April 25).

evidence.sourceName: Business Insider - Stevie Nicks 2026 Tour
evidence.evidenceSnippet: 2025-2026 tour schedule with Billy Joel co-headlining stadium shows and solo An Evening with Stevie Nicks dates. Known for blending Fleetwood Mac staples with solo favorites.

evidence.sourceName: The Crimson - Stevie Nicks Concert Review
evidence.evidenceSnippet: 77-year-old singer performs with full backing band, opens with Not Fade Away cover, segues into Dreams, Stand Back, Landslide. Set includes 13 songs plus encores, nearly two hours long with slideshow tributes to Prince, Tom Petty, Hendrix, Dylan.

touringPackageDescription: Electronic music pioneer performs immersive quadraphonic live shows on her Buchla 200e modular synthesizer in intimate venues, churches, and cathedrals. Her performances are spatial sound experiences, with Ciani manipulating four-channel audio to create enveloping electronic soundscapes. Shows are part of her Reflections series and often feature supporting ambient artists. Set length is typically one extended performance of improvisational Buchla compositions.

evidence.sourceName: Avant Music News - Suzanne Ciani Reflections Tour
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Reflections Tour 2025-2026 immersed in quadraphonic and light with dates in Denver (Nov 15), San Francisco (Nov 21 at Grace Cathedral), and European dates.

evidence.sourceName: Resident Advisor - Suzanne Ciani Tour Dates
evidence.evidenceSnippet: 2026 dates include NU Festival (June) and Module Festival (August).

evidence.sourceName: Songkick - Suzanne Ciani 2026
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Tour dates in 2025-2026 including Brooklyn (Dec 2025), South American tour (Jan-Feb 2026).

touringPackageDescription: R&B superstar performs large-scale stadium and arena concerts as both headliner and co-headliner. Her shows feature theatrical set designs with elaborate props (including a giant ant), stunning visual backdrops, and extensive choreography. Setlists span her Grammy-winning albums SOS and Ctrl plus collaborations with Kendrick Lamar. The 2025 Grand National Tour was a massive stadium co-headlining run across North America.

evidence.sourceName: Ticketmaster - SZA Grand National Tour
evidence.evidenceSnippet: 2025 Grand National Tour with Kendrick Lamar: 23 stadium dates across US and Canada from April-June 2025 including MetLife Stadium, SoFi Stadium, Soldier Field.

evidence.sourceName: Pitchfork - Grand National Tour Live Review
evidence.evidenceSnippet: SZA's stage design genuinely out of this world - rides a massive ant prop, performs on floor extensively, hits mesmerizing vocal runs. Three-hour show with spectacular visual production.

evidence.sourceName: Ticketmaster Blog - SZA Grand National Tour
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Following SOS Tour (2023-2024), SZA embarked on Grand National Tour (2025). Past tours include Championship Tour (2018) and Ctrl the Tour (2017-2018).

touringPackageDescription: The Talking Heads officially disbanded in 1991 and have never reunited. However, former frontman David Byrne tours regularly with elaborate theatrical productions featuring his solo material and Talking Heads classics. His current Who Is The Sky tour (2025-2026) features 13 mobile musicians/dancers in choreographed performances with massive LED screen visuals, performing Byrne's new songs alongside Talking Heads hits like Once in a Lifetime, Psycho Killer, and Burning Down the House.

evidence.sourceName: Consequence of Sound - David Byrne 2026 Tour Dates
evidence.evidenceSnippet: David Byrne added 2026 dates in Asia (Singapore, Bangkok, Tokyo, Seoul) and North America (Hollywood Bowl, Forest Hills Stadium). Tour in support of Who Is The Sky album features 13 musicians, singers and dancers, all mobile throughout the set.

evidence.sourceName: Best Classic Bands - David Byrne 2025-2026 Tour
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Tour features members of the American Utopia band, all mobile throughout the set. Byrne refuses to reunite Talking Heads for a quick buck.

evidence.sourceName: Medium - David Byrne Who Is The Sky Tour Review
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Broadway-level theatrical show with four giant screens covering the stage, choreographed mobile band in blue uniforms, mixes Talking Heads classics with new material.

touringPackageDescription: Dutch audiovisual artist who creates real-time generative visuals using his self-programmed Versum software. His performances are immersive A/V collaborations where he responds live to musicians' sounds with projected 3D digital graphics. He has toured with Thom Yorke's Tomorrow's Modern Boxes, collaborated with Nicolas Jaar, Ben Frost, and Sote. Performances feature large-scale projections that transform venues into responsive visual environments.

evidence.sourceName: Sharjah Art Foundation - Sote and Tarik Barri
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Collaborative performance Nov 2025 with Sote: layered electronic sounds juxtaposed with large-scale generative visuals transforming Africa Hall into a responsive visual environment. Features real-time projections using Barri's self-built software.

evidence.sourceName: Le Guess Who - Tarik Barri Bio
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Barri is known for creating striking visuals for Thom Yorke and Nicolas Jaar. His live A/V presentations explore isolation through audiovisual performance.

evidence.sourceName: Reddit AMA - Tarik Barri
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Uses self-made Versum software to build overwhelming immersive universes of digital graphics, creates visuals live on stage imaging the nuances of sound to fracture and morph to music.

touringPackageDescription: Australian multi-instrumentalist performs mesmerizing solo looping shows, building entire songs layer by layer in real time using guitar, bass, drums, keys, saxophone, flute, trumpet, and vocals. The 2025-2026 Return to the Roots tour features both solo looping performances and band-augmented sets in theaters and at major festivals. Shows run 90 minutes to two hours with improvisational elements that make each performance unique.

evidence.sourceName: Tash Sultana Official - 2026 Tour
evidence.evidenceSnippet: 2026 North American tour includes BottleRock Napa Valley, Bonnaroo Music + Arts Festival, and headlining shows at theaters across US and Canada. 42 upcoming concerts across 15 countries.

evidence.sourceName: Ticketmaster - Tash Sultana 2026 Dates
evidence.evidenceSnippet: 2026 North American Tour: Riverside (May 24), Tucson (May 27), Sandy UT (May 30), St. Louis (June 2), Philadelphia (June 5), Boston (June 6), New York (June 7), Montreal (June 9), plus EU/UK dates in August and March 2027 arena tour.

evidence.sourceName: Rock DNA Magazine - Return to the Roots Review
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Tash builds entire songs via looping - truly incredible to watch them build from foundational beat to intricate vocals, harmonies, and all the magic in between. Wields alto sax, flute, drums, piano, trumpet, and array of guitars. 90-minute shows with improvisational nuances.
